data.title: Ependion Unit to Purchase Germany-based Welotec
data.description:
Ependion's Westermo business agreed to fully acquire German edge computing-focused group Welotec GmbH, according to a Wednesday release.

The Swedish technology group will pay an initial 35.2 million euros in cash for the deal, which will be partially funded through a 300 million-krona directed share issue, subject to conditions. A performance-based earn-out of up to an additional 14.8 million euros is also included in the deal.

Deal closing is expected in the second quarter, subject to customary completion conditions.

data.title: Netflix Expands Multilingual Options on Televisions
data.description:
Netflix said its full list of multilingual options was expanded for any title for television viewers, matching the service available on mobile devices and web browsers.

Almost a third of all viewing is for non-English stories, and "subtitles and dubbing are more important than ever," Netflix said Wednesday in a statement.

data.title: Expanse Studios (GMGI) Secures Brazilian iGaming Certification
data.description:
Las Vegas, Nevada--(Newsfile Corp. - April 2, 2025) - Expanse Studios, a subsidiary of Golden Matrix Group Inc. , has received the game certification for the Brazilian market. Issued by Gaming Laboratories International (GLI). The certification covers Expanse's proprietary crash game Super Heli, one of the company's best-performing titles globally. The game was certified under GLI Report Number MO-711-MEN-24-03-711, in full compliance with Brazil's federal iGaming technical standards.

To view the full announcement, including downloadable images, bios, and more, click here.

Key Takeaways:

  • Expanse Studios, a subsidiary of Golden Matrix Group , has received official certification for its top-performing crash game Super Heli in Brazil, issued by Gaming Laboratories International (GLI)
  • The expectation for 2025 is very optimistic, with projections to generate US$ 5.6 billion in gross gaming revenue (GGR) alone, according to data from H2 Gambling Capital.
  • This certification follows Golden Matrix's recently secured definitive license for Meridianbet's online and retail betting operations in Brazil

Click image above to view full announcement.

About Super Heli

Super Heli is an innovative crash-type iGaming title where players bet on a rising multiplier and attempt to cash out before it crashes.

“Our first certification in Brazil marks a significant milestone in our global expansion strategy,” said Damjan Stamenkovic, CEO of Expanse Studios. “Brazil’s regulated iGaming market offers substantial growth potential, and Super Heli’s certification opens the door for the broader deployment of our proprietary content. Together with Meridianbet’s definitive betting license, we are well-positioned to create long-term value for our shareholders in this important market.”

About Expanse Studios

Founded in 2017, Expanse Studios, part of Golden Matrix Group , is a leading B2B iGaming content provider specializing in slots, crash games, table games, and gamified casino experiences. With a portfolio of 55+ proprietary games, Expanse Studios delivers market-adapted content across regulated markets, sweepstakes platforms, and social casinos worldwide. For more information, visit expanse.studio.

About Golden Matrix Group

Golden Matrix Group is a developer and licensor of advanced online gaming platforms and content. The Company operates in regulated markets globally through its B2B and B2C business units, including Expanse Studios and Meridianbet. For more information visit goldenmatrix.com.

Contacts:

Milan Koricanac

milan.koricanac@meridianbet.com

Source: Meridianbet Group

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/247074

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Wednesday at 11 AM ET
data.description:
Tesla's Global Vehicle Deliveries Sank 13% in First Quarter

The decline is further evidence that a growing consumer backlash against the brand and CEO Elon Musk is hurting the automaker's business.

Rivian Had a Better Quarter Than Tesla. Its Stock Is Down Anyway.

The EV start-up reported first-quarter deliveries of 8,640 vehicles, down 36% year over year but in line with company and Wall Street estimates.

BlackBerry's stock heads for its worst day in more than a year after revenue outlook disappoints

Weighed down by BlackBerry's weaker-than-expected outlook, the stock is heading for a seventh straight loss.

DJT Stock Tumbles as Trump Media Files for Trump Trust to Sell $2.3 Billion in Shares

The Donald J. Trump Revocable Trust would be allowed to sell 114,750,000 shares.

Consulting Giants Offer Billions in Cuts to Federal Contracts. It Might Not Be Enough.

Accenture, Deloitte, IBM and other firms submitted proposed price concessions but are expected to be asked to dig deeper.

Trump to Review Preliminary TikTok Deal As Deadline Approaches

Senior administration officials are expected to attend a Wednesday meeting about a potential deal; plan would require Beijing's blessing.

Johnson & Johnson's Talc Defeat Caps Bankruptcy-Court Backlash on Torts

J&Js failure to win approval of an $8 billion bankruptcy plan for its talc liabilities is the latest court ruling to pare back the use of chapter 11 to resolve mass torts and could limit the appeal of bankruptcy for companies looking to drive settlements.

Rio Tinto Braces for Investor Vote on Review of London Listing

Thursday's vote in London at Rio Tinto's annual shareholder meeting will test support for a change that has been championed by activist investor Palliser Capital.

Credit Agricole Gets ECB Nod to Raise BPM Stake as UniCredit Readies Bid Launch

The French bank said that the ECB on Tuesday authorized Credit Agricole to cross the 10% threshold in the Italian bank's share capital and to hold up to a 19.9% stake in Banco BPM.

Deutsche Bank's DWS Fined $27 Million in Greenwashing Probe

The asset-management arm of Deutsche Bank agreed to settle an investigation by German prosecutors into allegations that it overstated its environmental, social and corporate-governance practices.

data.title: AtriCure Price Target Maintained With a $60.00/Share by Citizens Capital Markets
RBC Revises Pandora Price Target, Earnings Forecasts on Commodity Price Risks
data.description:
RBC Capital Markets on Wednesday trimmed Pandora's price target to 1,050 Danish kroner from 1,100 kroner and adjusted its forecasts, citing risks to its future margin and earnings amid rising commodity prices.

The research firm, which has an underperform rating on the stock, expects the Danish jewelry maker to report a 5.5% organic group revenue growth to 7.28 billion kroner in the first quarter.

"Pandora's underlying revenue momentum should remain healthy in 1Q25E with the ability to deliver mid-single digit retail LFL (noting +5% price contribution) which is encouraging in the context of the broader consumer environment," analysts said. "However, we note potential downside risk to FY26E margin outcomes due to persistent silver/gold price appreciation (additional 100-120bps gross margin headwinds) adding to the unmitigated 130bps margin pressure already flagged by management. Lowering FY26E-27E earnings by 7-8%, and RBC estimates are 12-14% below consensus."

Analysts added that key areas to watch for at Pandora's first quarter results release on May 7 include current trading trends in key markets, US tariffs and cost-saving initiatives.

data.title: Why an SEC Filing Is Causing DJT Stock to Fall
data.description:
By Mackenzie Tatananni

Shares of Trump Media & Technology Group tumbled Wednesday after the company filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission to allow a trust headed by President Donald Trump to sell around $2.3 billion in stock.

Shares of the social media company declined 7.8% to $18.69 after Trump Media filed a Form S-3 with the SEC. The registration statement would allow shareholders to periodically sell stock, but doesn't guarantee that the shares actually will be sold.

The form includes mention of 114,750,000 shares held by the Donald J. Trump Revocable Trust. Of those, 78,750,000 are common stock, while the remainder are so-called earnout shares issued to Trump on April 26, 2024. The filing noted that the president is the trust's sole beneficiary.

This is breaking news. Check back for updates.

Write to Mackenzie Tatananni at mackenzie.tatananni@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Tariffs Could Accelerate Shift Away From U.S. EquitiesMarket Talk
data.description:
1116 ET - Trump's tariff policies have pressured U.S. equities as non-U.S. investors pull out, which could mark a "long-lasting turnaround in financial markets," French asset manager Ostrum says in a note. "Non-U.S. investors had bought the idea that a Trump win would mean a pro-business environment, pouring hundreds of billions into U.S. markets late last year," it says. These inflows are now reversing as tariff fears grow. The biggest losers are the 'Magnificent Seven' U.S. stocks of Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet, Meta, Nvidia and Tesla. Beyond these "megacap" stocks, nearly half of Russell 2000 companies is loss-making, Ostrum says. "U.S. exceptionalism seems to rely on a very narrow set of companies that are now under pressure." (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

1112 ET - The U.S. median home price is $412,000, requiring 10 workdays monthly to afford the mortgage, says Realtor.com, but for residents in some states, the number of workdays required can go up to 17 days. Hawaii's $796,947 median demands 17 days for a $5,222 payment, while California--$4,773--and Montana--$613,275--need 15 days each, driven by demand and rising costs in places like Bozeman. Conversely, the Midwest and Southeast offer relief. West Virginia--$247,000--and Ohio--$259,450--require just seven and six days, respectively. Other affordable states--Kansas, Missouri, Indiana, Illinois, and Michigan--also average a week's work for mortgage payments, highlighting stark regional disparities in housing affordability. (chris.wack@wsj.com)

1058 ET - Municipal bonds are "very attractive," Chilton Trust's Tim Horan says, referring to how close munis yields are to their comparable Treasurys. The 30-year AAA munis yield is more than 90% the yield on the 30-year Treasury, according to FactSet. Horan says that percentage is usually lower. "We haven't seen some of these ratios for quite some time," he says. Considering that most munis are tax-exempt, investors facing high federal income taxes could get a better after-tax return on munis. He likes seven- to 10-year munis. He says supply has increased, weighing down on muni prices, making the asset class "more attractive than what it has been." (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1055 ET - Tariff concerns have driven up market-based inflation expectations over the next year. But farther out, they are still fairly constrained, indicating that many investors think that the impact could be relatively short lived, analysts at Deutsche Bank note. Going by the CPI swaps market, one-year inflation expectations are in the 99th percentile of their levels over the past couple years, at around 3% or so. But expectations for inflation between mid-2026 and mid-2027 are only at the 60th percentile of levels in recent years. And inflation expectations for the late 2020s and the early 2030s remain barely elevated compared with where they have traded since 2023. "(matt.grossman@wsj.com; @mattgrossman)

1051 ET - Redfin commissioned a survey of 500 real-estate agents, revealing what is calls the challenges and opportunities facing today's real estate professionals. Only 21.2% would recommend a career as an agent, with 49.8% unlikely to recommend it, citing unpredictable income and client scarcity as drawbacks, though entrepreneurial freedom was a highlight. The affordability crisis tops future concerns, with 64.2% of agents worried, alongside inventory shortages and falling commissions. Following a year of industry upheaval, 51% of agents now hold an unfavorable view of the National Association of Realtors, a significant increase from 19% in 2023, Redfin says. "A substantial percentage (38%) believe the NAR settlement changes have negatively impacted their business, compared to 5.2% who noted a positive impact," Redfin adds. (chris.wack@wsj.com)

0946 ET - Nail-biting ahead of the tariffs announcement keeps the dollar down, as Wall Street braces for potential economic headwinds. Trump's expected reciprocal levies are to be revealed in the afternoon. "A wide range of outcomes are possible, and there are several key dimensions to the announcement that we'll be watching," Goldman Sachs economists write. They will be watching for the full list of countries included, the magnitude of the average tariff rate and the timeline for implementation. The WSJ Dollar Index falls 0.2% to 99.50, slightly above this year's low. The greenback weakens 0.2% versus the euro and 0.1% against the yen. It is up 0.2% against the Canadian dollar. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0922 ET - Liberation Day might not deliver the certainty that investors desire, argues Karl Schamotta, the Toronto-based chief market strategist at forex firm Corpay. He predicts the formal paperwork accompanying President Trump's tariff announcement are unlikely to contain the details and clear-cut instructions for full implementation. "Major areas of ambiguity will persist long after the announcement," Schamotta says. He's advising clients that the foreign-exchange response will likely prove "complex and counterintuitive." (Paul.Vieira@wsj.com, @paulvieira)

0920 ET - Crude futures are lower with the market awaiting U.S. inventory data and major tariff announcements from the White House. "Price trends may be upended after the market digests tariffs and their impact on the crude oil market today," Alex Hodes of StoneX says in a note. Analysts note a large and unexpected 6 million barrel increase in U.S. crude stocks reported yesterday by the API that's also weighing on prices. Closely watched EIA data are due at 10:30 a.m. ET, with analysts in a Wall Street Journal survey forecasting a 700,000 barrel draw. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

0918 ET - March payrolls, due Friday, are likely to show the U.S. labor market weakening, but not fast enough to fuel recession fears, Pantheon's Oliver Allen writes. He expects a 125,000 increase in private payrolls, down from 140,000 in February. The figure would be "weaker than the recent trend, but likely strong enough to ease fears of an imminent recession," Allen says. Meanwhile, ADP reports an addition of 155,000 private-sector jobs last month, beating forecasts. Allen dismisses the ADP figure as a poor predictor of payrolls. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0906 ET - Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney holds an advantage over his main political rival on all the 5 top issues identified by voters as paramount, says Angus Reid Institute, the polling organization. Perhaps of most importance for this race, Angus Reid says, is the 65-point lead he holds over Conservative Party Leader Pierre Poilievre on which leader is best suited to handling President Donald Trump. Carney, the former central banker, also leads in Angus Reid polling when it comes to who is most trusted on dealing with inflation, health care, managing the economy and addressing housing affordability. The polling comes on the same day that Trump is expected to roll out hefty tariffs on imports from most of America's closest trading partners. Carney is seeking a mandate to govern in an April 28 election. (Paul.Vieira@wsj.com; @paulvieira)

0852 ET - Wall Street remains anxious about the Trump tariffs announcement later today, with investors taking refuge in Treasurys. As a result, yields keep falling, even after a hot employment report. ADP says in March private-sector employers added 155,000 jobs, up from an upwardly revised 84,000 in February. Economists surveyed by WSJ expected 120,000. February factory orders, due at 10 a.m. ET, are expected to cool down. The 10-year is at 4.134% and the two-year at 3.857%, which would be their lowest settle since October. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0825 ET - Increased competition among private-credit firms vying to finance buyouts is likely to encourage those firms to expand into less crowded areas such as investment-grade debt and asset-backed financing, according to Carlyle. "A competitive equilibrium seems to have been established in deal finance markets that suggests further market share gains may be difficult to achieve without potentially sacrificing the return premium," Carlyle's Mark Jenkins and Jason Thomas write in a report. "Look for the private-credit industry to grow its footprint in less penetrated sectors eager to explore alternatives to one-size-fits-all public markets." Jenkins leads Carlyle's global credit group, while Thomas is the asset manager's head of global research and investment strategy. (luis.garcia@wsj.com; @lhgarcia)

data.title: POLONIA Rate rises 0.02 pp.

WARSAW, Apr 2 (Reuters) – POLONIA the reference rate for Overnight deposits amounted to 5.55 percent.The volume of transactions concluded till 16:30 by banks participating in POLONIA fixing amounted to 1,500 mln PLN.

data.title: Dunav Osiguranje drags down Belgrade bourse BELEX15
data.description:
BELGRADE (Serbia), April 2 (SeeNews) - The blue-chip index of the Belgrade Stock Exchange, the BELEX15, shed 0.16% on Wednesday, closing at 1,143.51, pushed down by losses in insurer Dunav Osiguranje [BEL:DNOS], bourse data showed.

Dunav, the only blue-chip decliner on the day, fell by 1.05% to 1,514 dinars at close as 1,435 shares traded. The insurer generated the single largest turnover of 2.2 million dinars for the day.

Civil engineering company Energoprojekt Holding [BEL:ENHL] was the only blue-chip gainer, advancing by 2.18% to close at 422 dinars in a trade of 187 shares.

Aerodrom Nikola Tesla [BEL:AERO] closed unchanged at 2,000 dinars as four shares changed hands.

No other blue-chip companies traded on Wednesday.

The broader BELEXline, a free-float market capitalisation-weighted index, closed virtually flat, at 2,500.65 points, compared to 2,501.57 points on Tuesday.

Total turnover on the Belgrade bourse soared to 306.3 million dinars ($2.8 million/ 2.6 million euro) from 6.6 million dinars on Tuesday, mainly due to trading in government paper, which contributed some 303.6 million dinars of the total on Wednesday, compared to 1.7 million dinars the day before.

(1 euro = 117.197 dinars)

data.title: Johnson & Johnson's Talc Litigation Risks 'Likely' Manageable, RBC Says
data.description:
Johnson & Johnson's talc litigation risks are "likely" manageable as the drugmaker is going back to the tort system to fight what it deems "junk science," RBC Capital Markets said in a note Monday.

The company said late Monday that the US Bankruptcy Court for the Southern District of Texas denied its subsidiary Red River Talc's request to confirm its proposed prepackaged bankruptcy plan.

RBC said Johnson & Johnson's return to the tort system is "prudent" because it has had prior success there, winning 16 of 17 cases in the last 11 years. The firm also noted that the company will do the Daubert Challenge that places the burden on the plaintiff to prove the scientific validity of their opinion, which could potentially end federal cases.

RBC added that the company has a "strong" cash position and hasn't changed its 2025 guidance.

The firm maintained an outperform rating on the stock with a $181 price target.

Shares were up 1.4% in recent trading.

data.title: Macy's Recovering Over $600,000 in 'Erroneously Awarded' Bonuses to Executives
data.description:
Macy's said it's recovering more than $600,000 in cash bonuses "erroneously awarded" to its senior executives following an accounting issue, according to a regulatory filing Tuesday.

The company said in the filing that the executives' bonuses were linked to the 2023 adjusted EBITDA that was overstated by about $81.1 million.

Bonuses to executives under the company's 2023 annual incentive plan should have been $3,541,659 and not $4,151,272 million due to the erroneously awarded compensation of $609,613, Macy's said.

As of April 1, the company said $352,093 remained outstanding and that it will seek to recover the amount during fiscal 2025.

data.title: Democrat Xavier Becerra launches run for California governor
data.description:
Democrat Xavier Becerra on Wednesday launched a run for governor of California, potentially pitting himself against former Vice President Kamala Harris.

California voters in 2026 race will select a governor to succeed Democrat Gavin Newsom, who has held the post since 2019 but cannot run for re-election due to term limits.

Becerra, 67, served as health and human services director under former President Joe Biden, and was the first Latino to hold the office.

"I watched my parents, a construction worker and a clerical worker, achieve the California Dream. Can we do that today with this affordability crisis? ... We can do that but you need a leader who can be tough," Becerra said in a video announcing his run.

A Sacramento native, he became a state legislator in 1990 before representing the state in the U.S. Congress for more than two decades and later becoming the state's attorney general.

Harris, who was also California's attorney general and a U.S. senator before becoming vice president, has told people she intends to decide later this year whether to run for California governor, Politico reported last month, citing sources.

Former U.S. Representative Katie Porter launched a run for governor in March.
